messages.content:
Which of the following is false?

Because of greenhouse gases, Earth's surface can absorb the incoming long-wavelength solar radiation and emit the infrared radiation back.

The greenhouse effect existed before industrialization in the 1800s.

Climate change is a century-long directional trend.

All options are correct.

Climate variability is the inter-annual variations in climate, which equals the trend of climate change.

Removal of forests weakens Earth's ability to absorb carbon dioxide from the atmosphere.

Climate variability is the inter-annual variations in climate, which equals the trend of climate change. (This statement is false - climate variability refers to short-term fluctuations in weather patterns, while climate change refers to long-term changes in global temperature, sea level, etc.)

Which of the following climate change impacts may NOT cause coral bleaching?

Altered ocean current

Water pollution

Ocean acidification

All factors may cause coral bleaching.

Increasing water temperature

Sea level rise
All of the listed factors, except water pollution, may cause coral bleaching.

The atmosphere on Earth is warmed because  ________.

vegetation can release CO2

fossil fuels release heat during burning

molecules in the atmosphere can absorb radiation from Earth

vegetation can absorb CO2

None is correct
Molecules in the atmosphere can absorb radiation from Earth, causing the atmosphere on Earth to warm.

Which of the following factor may NOT cause the major loss of forested areas?

Limited rights and involvement of forest-dependent communities

Energy poverty issues in rural areas

Depreciation of forest ecosystem services

Tenure insecurity of logging companies

Trophy hunting activities led by the indigenous people

All factors can significantly cause forest loss.

Improper legislation and enforcement on forest conservation

Timber production and trade that are outside of legal boundaries
Trophy hunting activities led by the indigenous people is less likely to cause major loss of forested areas compared to the other factors listed.

Which of the following statement is NOT correct?

The majority of forest gain in recent years occurred in tropical areas due to many reforestation programs around the world.

The loss of forested areas in many places is mainly due to logging, industrialization, urbanization, and climate change.

Most primary forests of native species in the world are affected by human activities.

Less than 10% of forested areas are not naturally regenerated.

All options are correct.

On average, approximately 3 million hectares of trees are regenerated annually.

On average, approximately 3 million hectares of trees are regenerated annually. (This statement is not correct - the actual annual rate of global tree cover gain is estimated to be around 7.6 million hectares, according to satellite data.)

Nowadays, deforestation issues occur at the fastest rate in ________.
boreal forests

temperate deciduous forests

All options are correct.

temperate rain forests

tropical rain forests
Deforestation issues occur at the fastest rate in tropical rain forests.

Which of the following option is NOT one of the global objectives on forests according to Global Forest Resources Assessment 2010?

All options are correct.

Enhance forest-based economic, social and environmental benefits, including by improving the livelihoods of forest-dependent people.

Encourage public resources from limited sources for the implementation of sustainable forest management.

Reverse the decline in official development assistance for sustainable forest management.

Significantly decrease the area of unprotected forests worldwide.

Improve the gain of forest cover worldwide via sustainable forest management (e.g. protection, restoration, etc.).
Significantly decrease the area of unprotected forests worldwide. (This is not one of the global objectives on forests according to the Global Forest Resources Assessment 2010)

Which of the following is NOT true of scenario planning? 
  We can use scenario planning can assess future outcomes. 
  None is correct. 
  We need to assess how well our management assumptions and priorities meet the objectives. 
  The use of computer simulation models can help generate scenarios. 
  Adjustment of the planning is unnecessary when the management objectives are not met. 
  It allows for the risk and uncertainty.

Adjustment of the planning is unnecessary when the management objectives are not met. (This is not true of scenario planning as it involves assessing potential future outcomes and adjusting management actions based on the results.)

Which of the following remote sensing system would be most appropriate for monitoring the long-term trend of landscape changes (e.g. over 40 years) across different nations?

MODIS

Landsat

Both Landsat and MODIS

Google Earth

UAV and Digital photogrammetry

RADAR

Fengyun-2

None of the options

Landsat would be most appropriate for monitoring the long-term trend of landscape changes (e.g. over 40 years) across different nations since it has been operational since 1972, which allows for a comprehensive long-term monitoring program.

Which of the following statement is false?

All options are correct.

Human eyes can only see a small proportion of the electromagnetic spectrum, especially the visible section.

Remote sensing measurements can be taken from centimetres to kilometres.

Photography was invented before aerial images were taken from balloons and birds.

Vegetation and dark soil can be separated via Landsat multispectral scanner at 2000 nm wavelength.

Turbid water and dark soil may be differentiated by Landsat multispectral scanner from the 800 to 900 nm wavelength.

Nectar in flowers may be seen as dark blue areas for honey bees.

Vegetation and dark soil can be separated via Landsat multispectral scanner at 2000 nm wavelength. (This statement is false - Landsat multispectral scanner only measures wavelengths up to 2.5 μm, which is in the near-infrared region of the spectrum. Vegetation and dark soil can be separated by the normalized difference vegetation index (NDVI), which uses reflectance measurements in the red and near-infrared regions.)

Which of the following statement is incorrect?

Satellite-based biomass data may not always lead to precise monitoring of vegetation.

All options are correct.

Vegetation phenology can be assessed via remotely sensed Leaf Area Index.

Vegetation indices can be used to monitor the inter-annual variations of plant communities over time.

Vegetation indices can be used to examine forest health conditions but not forest fire impacts.

Satellite-based chlorophyll concentrations can be used to determine the strength of photosynthesis on Earth's surface.

Vegetation indices can be used to examine forest health conditions but not forest fire impacts. (This statement is incorrect - vegetation indices can be used to monitor vegetation properties including health, biomass, and changes in the canopy associated with wildfires.)

In what part of the spectrum is the energy from the sun the highest?

X-ray

Mid-infrared

Gamma-ray

Both ultraviolet and infra-red

Near-infrared

Visible

Ultraviolet
The energy from the sun is highest in the visible spectrum, specifically in the blue and green wavelengths.

On a computer screen, what colour will healthy vegetation appear if the mid-infrared band is displayed as red, the near-infrared band is displayed as green, and the red band is displayed as blue?

Red

Green

Purple

None of the options

Blue

Yellow

Orange
Healthy vegetation will appear red on a computer screen if the mid-infrared band is displayed as red, the near-infrared band is displayed as green, and the red band is displayed as blue.

Which of the following statement is NOT true?

All options are true.

3D driver simulation allows users to model and experience potential pathways in different landscapes.

SIMFOR models can create layers on top of forest stands to indicate the condition of habitats.

Models of GIS can degrade the visual quality of landscapes if inefficient algorithms are used.

3D driver simulation allows forest managers to practically examine the configurations and uniformity of a forest stand.

Global applications of GIS include carbon mapping, sea level mapping, and vegetation mapping.

Models of GIS can degrade the visual quality of landscapes if inefficient algorithms are used. (This statement is not true - models and algorithms of GIS have no direct impact on the visual quality of landscapes. However, the choice of visualization techniques can affect the way data is interpreted and presented.)

Which of the following statement is wrong?
To determine the visual magnitude, slope differences, aspect differences, and 3D rendering need to be considered.

All options are correct.

Digital terrain models in GIS may not show the oblique viewpoint of a landscape.

Visual magnitude analysis improves the understanding of topographical studies via different algorithms.

Oblique view of landscapes provides key information in visual magnitude.

Vectors of perspective view and surface are used to calculate the Euclidean distance between two points.

Multiple viewpoints can be evaluated in the visual magnitude analysis.

Visual magnitude analysis improves the understanding of topographical studies via different algorithms. (This statement is wrong - visual magnitude analysis provides a better understanding of the visual impact of terrain features and changes on people, rather than on topographical studies.)

A GIS model is a complex representation of a phenomenon or system on the Earth's surface.

True

False
True.

Which of the following statement is correct?

All options are correct.

Non-spatial information is unnecessary in GIS analysis.

GIS provides methods to manipulate data in a cell individually or based on the attributes of neighboring cells.

Compared to GPS and Remote Sensing, field survey can be unnecessary and ineffective.

GIS can help analyze forest inventory and forest health, but not stand-level forest structures.

GIS allows for the analysis of GPS and remote sensing data, but not the storage of them.
GIS provides methods to manipulate data in a cell individually or based on the attributes of neighboring cells.

Which of the following is incorrect?

Applications used by the computer to perform special analysis is the software of GIS.

People are at the core of major GIS components.

Model surface can also be essential for building structures in GIS analysis.

All options are correct.

The physical element that makes up a computer system of GIS is its hardware.

Methods used produce correct reproducible results are referred to as procedures in GIS components.

GIS has two types of data models only (i.e. raster and vector).

All options, except the last one, are correct. GIS data models include multiple types, such as raster, vector, and others like TIN (triangulated irregular network) and DEM (digital elevation model), among others.

Which of the following climate change impacts may NOT cause coral bleaching?

Altered ocean current

Sea level rise

Water pollution

All factors may cause coral bleaching.

Ocean acidification

Increasing water temperature

Sea level rise may not directly cause coral bleaching, as it does not affect water temperature, which is the main cause of coral bleaching. However, sea level rise can increase sedimentation, reduce water quality, and exacerbate storm surges, which can indirectly contribute to coral bleaching.

Developed countries like the United States of America should enact laws to slow down climate change because  ________.

global climate change effects are most significant in developed countries

they are the major sources of the issue (e.g. massive carbon emission since the industrialization)

no other countries have initiated efforts to slow global climate change

All options are correct

their industries support the legislation
They are the major sources of the issue (e.g. massive carbon emission since the industrialization).

Forests in Canada do NOT include ________ forests.

subalpine

second-growth

deciduous

mangrove

old-growth
Forests in Canada do NOT include mangrove forests.

Illegal logging in forested areas significantly drives the loss of forests worldwide. The expansion of agricultural production is also a major contributor to forest degradation.
True

False
True.

Which of the following factor may NOT cause the major loss of forested areas?

Energy poverty issues in rural areas

All factors can significantly cause forest loss.

Depreciation of forest ecosystem services

Tenure insecurity of logging companies

Timber production and trade that are outside of legal boundaries

Improper legislation and enforcement on forest conservation

Trophy hunting activities led by the indigenous people

Limited rights and involvement of forest-dependent communities
Trophy hunting activities led by the indigenous people may not cause the major loss of forested areas. It is less likely to be a major contributor compared to the other factors listed.

Which of the following option is NOT one of the global objectives on forests according to Global Forest Resources Assessment 2010?

Encourage public resources from limited sources for the implementation of sustainable forest management.

Enhance forest-based economic, social and environmental benefits, including by improving the livelihoods of forest-dependent people.

Reverse the decline in official development assistance for sustainable forest management.

Significantly decrease the area of unprotected forests worldwide.

All options are correct.

Improve the gain of forest cover worldwide via sustainable forest management (e.g. protection, restoration, etc.).
Significantly decrease the area of unprotected forests worldwide is NOT one of the global objectives on forests according to the Global Forest Resources Assessment 2010.

Which of the following factors may NOT contribute to the recently-observed catastrophic forest fires in the United States of America (e.g. California), Brazil, and Australia?

accumulation of deadwood

prescribed burns

fire suppression

climate change and carbon emission

All options are correct

Prescribed burns may not contribute to the recently-observed catastrophic forest fires. In fact, prescribed burns can be a way to prevent catastrophic wildfires by reducing excess fuel and maintaining healthy forests.

Considering the objectives of forest management, which of the following is a forest management technique that involves systematically testing different management approaches and learning from approaches that work or don't work?

Multiple-use management (e.g. pulp logs, saw logs, house logs, etc.)

Timber harvesting with maximum yield

None is correct

Adaptive management (e.g. site evaluation and treatment)

Salvage logging (i.e. removing damaged trees due to disturbances)

Adaptive management (e.g. site evaluation and treatment) involves systematically testing different forest management approaches and learning from approaches that work or don't work, and is often used in sustainable forestry to adapt to changing conditions and ensure long-term forest health.

Which of the following is NOT true of scenario planning?

We can use scenario planning can assess future outcomes.

We need to assess how well our management assumptions and priorities meet the objectives.

The use of computer simulation models can help generate scenarios.

None is correct.

It allows for the risk and uncertainty.

Adjustment of the planning is unnecessary when the management objectives are not met.

Adjustment of the planning is unnecessary when the management objectives are not met. (This is not true of scenario planning - adjusting management actions based on the results of scenario planning is a key component of the process.)

How to control root disease?

Spread antibacterial drugs around the infected area

Thinning the crowns and let the sun light in

Pull out stumps and expose roots

Burn all the infected stumps

All options are correct.

Thinning the crowns and let the sun light in is a method to control root disease, but spread antibacterial drugs around the infected area, pull out stumps and expose roots, and burning infected stumps are not effective methods to control root disease.

Which is NOT the symptoms of tree disease I?

Stand has large openings

The tree produces less cone

The needle of trees is shedding and falling off

New species come into an ecosystem

All options are correct.
All options are correct.

Which statement is NOT true for a healthy forest?

There are different criteria from different aspect

Canker disease often happens in the old trees

Human can impact the forest health by bringing exotic pathogens

The special scale can influence the evaluation criteria

None is correct.

Canker disease often happens in the old trees is NOT true for a healthy forest - cankers are areas of diseased tissue typically caused by fungal or bacterial pathogens and can indicate a decline in forest health.

"Healthy" forests  ______.
are not associated with the fragility of ecosystems

need abiotic disturbances (e.g. wind, fire, etc.) only

are also affected by disturbance agents (e.g. insects, invasive species, etc.)

cannot be influenced by poor air quality

All options are correct

Healthy forests are also affected by disturbance agents (e.g. insects, invasive species, etc.) and can be associated with the fragility of ecosystems. They also require a balance of abiotic disturbances and need good air quality to function properly. Therefore, all options are correct.

Which statement of nutrient cycling is wrong?

Essential and limiting nutrients retained most strongly in late-succession

Nutrient will be tied up in biomass

All options are correct

Nutrient cycling usually starts with disturbances

Eventually nutrient outputs will be equal to the inputs of nutrient
All options, except "Nutrient cycling usually starts with disturbances," are correct. Nutrient cycling can start at any stage of ecosystem development and is not necessarily related to disturbances.
